steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Text 2 argues that Luttrell’s conflict with Sempringham Priory stemmed from a property dispute and that he was taking property he thought he had a right — this shows he believed his actions were justified.

Choice D directly reflects this idea by saying a fair assessment must consider Luttrell’s belief that his actions were justified. The other three either ignore this justification, introduce unrelated points, or misrepresent the historian’s view. That matches choice D — D is correct.
steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
